title: [Bug]: podman containers cannot be accessed from external network
body:
### Issue Description

I have Windows 10 host system with WSL2, hosting a Linux VM (Fedora CoreOS instantiated by podman as podman machine), and podman client running on Windows 10 host.  Created a sample container using command:

podman run -dp 9800:80/tcp --name webserver quay.io/libpod/banner:latest

From web browser, I can access localhost:9800, but times out when I try to access ip:9800, port 9800 is allowed in Windows defender firewall for inbound access for Domain, private and public networks.

When I view open ports in the system, I see port opened in 127.0.0.1:9800 by wslhost.exe, but no open ports in either 0.0.0.0 or <ip>.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman machine init
2. podman machine start
3. podman run -dp 9800:80/tcp --name webserver quay.io/libpod/banner:latest

podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                         COMMAND               CREATED        STATUS            PORTS                 NAMES
f6ad583b00c1  quay.io/libpod/banner:latest  nginx -g daemon o...  6 seconds ago  Up 6 seconds ago  0.0.0.0:9800->80/tcp  webserver

4. Access localhost:9800 from browser

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/122226346/211211385-a46b6f00-0698-4f82-80af-163599d0117c.png)

5. Access ip:9800 from browser

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/122226346/211211404-f68b2ea8-6c5d-42a0-91aa-d2b909620b19.png)

6. Output from netstat:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/122226346/211211431-89da6748-1018-48b5-8f96-7bf42b7809e2.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/122226346/211211455-ea85ceb5-705b-4c8e-a0b8-62db0c3910e9.png)



### Describe the results you received

Not able to access container network from external network, timeout error is seen.  Changing port number does not resolve the issue.

### Describe the results you expected

Container network is accessed from external network after port mapping.

comments.body:
IP configuration in the host system:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/122226346/211211755-bb743ae0-2725-4e05-a558-aad09b6c15b0.png)
@n1hility is this expected behavior?  I would not think so but I know there are some caveats to port mapping, wsl, etc.

After reading https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman-for-windows.md again, I noticed below in port forwarding:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/122226346/211461426-a1e34319-3b14-4ebf-a497-62e904f53480.png)

Where it mentions ports are bound against local host (specifically), is this really the case?

@Luap99:

podman machine ssh ss -tulpn
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:63058' (ECDSA) to the list of known hosts.
Netid State  Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address:Port  Peer Address:PortProcess
tcp   LISTEN 0      4096         0.0.0.0:9800       0.0.0.0:*    users:(("rootlessport",pid=112,fd=11))
tcp   LISTEN 0      128          0.0.0.0:63058      0.0.0.0:*
tcp   LISTEN 0      4096            [::]:9800          [::]:*    users:(("rootlessport",pid=112,fd=12))
tcp   LISTEN 0      128             [::]:63058         [::]:*

C:\WINDOWS\system32>netstat -aonp tcp | findstr :9800
  TCP    127.0.0.1:9800         0.0.0.0:0              LISTENING       24708

C:\WINDOWS\system32>netstat -aonp tcp | findstr :63058
  TCP    127.0.0.1:63058        0.0.0.0:0              LISTENING       24708
OK so we are binded correctly to `0.0.0.0` and `::` in linux, this means it is something in the wsl layer that does not bind the correcr addresses on the windows side. cc @n1hility

Sorry for the delay replying. This is expected behavior for WSL since WSL traffic is isolated to a separate network interface from Windows applications (this is the vWSL interface). You can reach it via localhost (preferred) which is traffic forwarded, or the ip address assigned to the WSL distro (e.g. podman machine ssh ifconfig or wsl -d podman-machine-default ifconfig), although this one is temporary and changes. 

If you need the port to be remotely accessible to other systems on the lan you need to add an ip forward (see bottom section of https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/networking)
